AN EF2 TORNADO WITH WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 110-115 MPH TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF HIGHWAY 69 NEAR ITS INTERSECTION WITH MAYOR STREET. AS IT CROSSED HIGHWAY 69...THE TORNADO STRENGTHENED RESULTING IN MAJOR DAMAGE TO THE HIBBETT SPORTS AND DOLLAR TREE STORES. TREE AND ROOF DAMAGE TO HOMES WERE NOTED IN SUBDIVISIONS BETWEEN HIGHWAY 69 AND CR 431 BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF CR 431 AND PIERCE STREET. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 1 MILE...WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 400 YARDS.

EF2 DAMAGE WAS FOUND IN SMITH COUNTY BETWEEN FM 16 AND FM 2710 WHERE A CELL PHONE TOWER...A HOUSE...AND TWO MOBILE HOMES SUSTAINED MAJOR DAMAGE. AN OZARKA WATER BOTTLING PLANT NORTH OF HAWKINS SUSTAINED MAJOR ROOF DAMAGE. PATH LENGTH OF THIS TORNADO WAS 27.36 MILES...WITH A PATH WIDTH OF 1.88 MILES.

AN EF1 TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST WEST OF COUNTY ROAD 4000 ABOUT A MILE NORTH OF HOLMES LAKE. IT TRAVELED EAST-NORTHEAST WHERE IT INTERSECTED HIGHWAY 59 NEAR THE KARMA FARM. IT DAMAGED TWO HOMES THERE WHILE KNOCKING DOWN SEVERAL TREES. IT CONTINUED EAST-NORTHEAST WHERE IT CROSSED FM 1793 AND THEN COUNTY ROAD 2106. IT LIFTED JUST EAST OF THE INTERSECTION OF COUNTY ROAD 2107 AND BUCKHORN ROAD. TREES WERE TWISTED AND UPROOTED ALL ALONG THE PATH. MAX WIND SPEED WAS 110 MPH AND PATH LENGTH 6.63 MILES.
